spike_purpose: Extract per-bar block from v1.5.8 run_execution_loop into standalone evaluate_bar() callable to enable shared signal-generation between research and live runtimes without re-implementation. Mechanical lift only — no logic changes.
adds: evaluate_bar.py: standalone per-bar evaluator (lifted body of v1.5.8 for-loop)
BarState dataclass: cross-bar mutable state (was outer-scope locals in v1.5.8)
EngineConfig dataclass: resolved STRATEGY_SIGNATURE config (was inline setup in v1.5.8)
resolve_engine_config(): config resolver function (was inline lines 215-256 in v1.5.8)
finalize_force_close(): end-of-data force-close handler (was inline lines 646-694 in v1.5.8)
invariants: Byte-identical output to v1.5.8 for every strategy on identical input data
Canonical per-bar order (in-position): SL/TP -> partial -> stop_mutation -> check_exit
Partial guard: partial_taken == False AND bars_held >= 1 AND unrealized_r >= 1.0001
Stop mutation is monotone: long tightens up only, short tightens down only; silent reject otherwise
Entry market state initial_stop_price is FROZEN; stop_price_active is live-mutable

spike_acceptance_test.method: Run identical historical bar set through v1.5.8 and v1.5.9; diff outputs.
spike_acceptance_test.tolerance: ZERO. Diff must return zero output.
